childhood.familyBackground: Father Tim Dorsey was an engineer for a company developing mass spectrometers; mother Marcia Dorsey was a homemaker. Raised Catholic. Father frequently brought home old equipment, sparking Jack's curiosity and encouraging tinkering with gadgets. Family moved frequently due to father's work.
childhood.familyBackgroundKo: 아버지 팀 도르시는 질량 분석기를 개발하는 회사의 엔지니어였고, 어머니 마시아 도르시는 주부였다. 가톨릭으로 양육받았다. 아버지는 자주 낡은 장비를 집으로 가져와 잭의 호기심을 자극했고 기구를 손질하도록 장려했다. 아버지의 직업으로 인해 가족은 자주 이사했다.
childhood.education: Attended Bishop DuBourg High School, a private Catholic school known for academic rigor. Enrolled at University of Missouri-Rolla (1995) to study computer science and mathematics. Transferred to New York University (NYU) in 1997 to study fashion design and programming but dropped out before completing degree.
childhood.educationKo: 학문적 엄격함으로 알려진 사립 가톨릭 학교 비숍 듀부르그 고등학교에 입학. 1995년 미주리-롤라 대학에 컴퓨터과학과 수학 전공으로 입학. 1997년 뉴욕대학교(NYU)로 편입하여 패션 디자인과 프로그래밍을 공부했으나 학위를 마치기 전에 중퇴.
childhood.earlyLife: At age 14, father bought him an IBM computer which was instrumental in his self-education in programming. Spent countless hours teaching himself to code. At age 15, wrote dispatch software for taxicabs and firefighting services that remained in use for decades. Fascinated by mapping and coordinating vehicle fleets. Hacked into a dispatch company's security hole and got hired as a result.
childhood.earlyLifeKo: 14세에 아버지가 IBM 컴퓨터를 사줬고, 이는 프로그래밍 자학에 결정적인 역할을 함. 무수한 시간을 코딩을 배우는 데 투자했다. 15세에 택시와 소방 서비스용 디스패치 소프트웨어를 작성했는데, 수십 년 동안 사용되었다. 차량 함대 매핑과 조정에 매료되었다. 디스패치 회사의 보안 허점을 해킹했고, 그 결과로 채용되었다.

careerTimeline.event: Dropped out of New York University before completing degree in fashion design and programming
careerTimeline.eventKo: 패션 디자인과 프로그래밍 학위를 마치기 전에 뉴욕대학교 중퇴

careerTimeline.event: Conceived the idea for a web-based realtime status/short message communication service inspired by dispatch systems, LiveJournal, and AOL Instant Messenger
careerTimeline.eventKo: 디스패치 시스템, 라이브저널, AOL 인스턴트 메신저에서 영감을 받아 웹 기반 실시간 상태/단문 통신 서비스 아이디어 구상

careerTimeline.event: Co-founded Twitter in March 2006 with Noah Glass, Biz Stone, and Evan Williams at Odeo
careerTimeline.eventKo: 2006년 3월 노아 글래스, 비즈 스톤, 에반 윌리엄스와 함께 오데오에서 트위터 공동 설립

careerTimeline.event: Posted the world's first tweet on March 21, 2006: 'just setting up my twttr'
careerTimeline.eventKo: 2006년 3월 21일 세계 최초의 트윗 게시: '방금 twttr 설정 중'

careerTimeline.event: Became CEO of Twitter and served until 2008
Co-founded Square (now Block) with Jim McKelvey after McKelvey shared story of lost $3,000 glass faucet sale due to inability to process card payments
careerTimeline.eventKo: 짐 맥켈비가 신용카드 결제 처리 불가로 인한 $3,000 유리 수도꼭지 판매 손실 이야기를 공유한 후 짐 맥켈비와 함께 스퀘어(현재 블록) 공동 설립

careerTimeline.event: Square launched as a mobile payments platform enabling small businesses to accept credit card payments
careerTimeline.eventKo: 스퀘어를 모바일 결제 플랫폼으로 출시하여 소규모 기업이 신용카드 결제를 수락할 수 있게 함

careerTimeline.event: Became a billionaire when venture capital investment valued Square at $3.2 billion in July 2012. By November, had net worth of $12.9 billion
careerTimeline.eventKo: 2012년 7월 벤처캐피탈 투자로 스퀘어의 가치가 32억 달러로 평가되면서 억만장자가 됨. 11월까지 순자산 129억 달러에 도달

careerTimeline.event: Returned as CEO of Twitter. Square went public (IPO) with stock trading under ticker SQ
careerTimeline.eventKo: 트위터 CEO로 복귀. 스퀘어 기업공개(IPO), 티커 SQ로 거래 시작

careerTimeline.event: Square expanded with Cash App launch (2013) and continued expansion into peer-to-peer payments and Bitcoin buying/selling (2018)
careerTimeline.eventKo: 캐시 앱 출시(2013)와 P2P 결제 및 비트코인 매매(2018)로의 지속적인 확장으로 스퀘어 확대

careerTimeline.event: Integrated Bitcoin buying and selling into Square and Cash App, demonstrating commitment to cryptocurrency
careerTimeline.eventKo: 스퀘어와 캐시 앱에 비트코인 매매를 통합하여 암호화폐에 대한 약속을 보여줌

careerTimeline.event: Announced moving $1 billion of equity in Square (roughly one-third of his wealth) to Start Small LLC and relief programs, funding COVID-19 relief, girls' education, health, and universal basic income initiatives
careerTimeline.eventKo: 스퀘어 지분 10억 달러(자산의 약 1/3)를 스타트 스몰 LLC와 구제 프로그램으로 이전하여 COVID-19 구호, 여성 교육, 보건, 보편적 기본소득 사업 지원 발표

careerTimeline.event: Square acquired Afterpay for $29 billion, expanding fintech ecosystem
careerTimeline.eventKo: 스퀘어가 애프터페이를 290억 달러에 인수하여 핀테크 생태계 확장

careerTimeline.event: Resigned as Twitter CEO on November 29, 2021 to focus on Bitcoin and Block full-time
careerTimeline.eventKo: 2021년 11월 29일 트위터 CEO 사직, 비트코인과 블록에 전념하기 위해

careerTimeline.event: Square rebranded to Block to reflect broader mission beyond payments, including Bitcoin infrastructure (Spiral, TBD), music streaming (Tidal), and decentralized finance
careerTimeline.eventKo: 스퀘어를 블록으로 브랜드 변경, 결제를 넘어 비트코인 인프라(스파이럴, TBD), 음악 스트리밍(타이달), 분산 금융을 포함한 광범위한 미션 반영

careerTimeline.event: Cashed out approximately 18 million Twitter shares (2% of outstanding shares) for around $974 million when Elon Musk acquired Twitter at approximately $54.20 per share
careerTimeline.eventKo: 엘론 머스크가 트위터를 약 주당 $54.20에 인수할 때 약 1,800만 주 트위터 지분(발행주식의 2%)을 약 9억 7,400만 달러에 현금화

careerTimeline.event: Launched Bitchat, a peer-to-peer messaging app using Bluetooth mesh networking for connection without internet
careerTimeline.eventKo: 블루투스 메시 네트워킹을 사용하는 P2P 메시징 앱 비트챗 출시, 인터넷 없이도 연결 가능

careerTimeline.event: Announced backing for new app diVine based on defunct Vine platform
careerTimeline.eventKo: 폐지된 바인 플랫폼 기반의 새로운 앱 디바인 지원 발표

failures.description: Served as CEO of both Twitter and Square simultaneously from 2015 to 2021, creating significant leadership concerns. Activist investor Elliott Management criticized his dual roles in early 2020. Employees reported Dorsey as 'hands-off to the extreme' and disengaged.
failures.descriptionKo: 2015년부터 2021년까지 트위터와 스퀘어의 CEO를 동시에 역임하여 상당한 리더십 우려 야기. 행동주의 투자자 엘리엇 매니지먼트가 2020년 초 그의 이중 역할을 비판. 직원들은 도르시를 '극도로 손떼는 식'이고 무관심하다고 보고.
failures.lesson: Divided attention and simultaneous leadership of multiple companies can compromise effectiveness. Full commitment to mission requires undivided focus.
failures.lessonKo: 분산된 관심과 여러 회사의 동시 리더십은 효과성을 해칠 수 있다. 미션에 대한 완전한 헌신은 집중된 초점이 필요하다.

failures.description: Made critical content moderation decisions at Twitter, including Trump account suspension, which Dorsey later acknowledged was 'the right thing for the public company business but wrong thing for the internet and society.' Burdened the company with too much power over public discourse.
failures.descriptionKo: 트럼프 계정 정지를 포함한 트위터의 중요한 콘텐츠 검증 결정. 도르시는 나중에 이것이 '공개 회사 비즈니스로는 올바른 것이었지만 인터넷과 사회로는 잘못된 것'이라고 인정. 회사에 공론의 장에 대한 너무 많은 권력을 부여함.
failures.lesson: Over-investing in company tools for managing public conversation gives platforms too much power and opens them to significant outside pressure. Better to build tools for users to manage themselves.
failures.lessonKo: 공개 대화 관리를 위한 회사 도구에 과도하게 투자하면 플랫폼에 너무 많은 권력을 부여하고 상당한 외부 압력에 노출된다. 사용자가 자신을 관리할 수 있는 도구를 구축하는 것이 낫다.

failures.description: Early Twitter technical problems: The product went down frequently in early days because decisions were based on guesses and intuition rather than balanced approach with data, leading to 'Fail Whale' incidents.
failures.descriptionKo: 초기 트위터 기술 문제: 제품이 초기에 자주 작동 불능이 되었는데, 의사결정이 추측과 직관에 기반하고 데이터를 이용한 균형잡힌 접근이 없어서, '실패한 고래' 사건을 야기함.
failures.lesson: Intuition alone is insufficient for scaling technology. Need balance between intuition and rigorous data analysis to ensure reliability.
failures.lessonKo: 직관만으로는 기술 확장에 불충분하다. 안정성을 보장하기 위해 직관과 엄격한 데이터 분석 사이의 균형이 필요하다.

quotes.text: The greatest lesson that I learned in all of this is that you have to start. Start now, start here, and start small.
quotes.textKo: 내가 배운 가장 큰 교훈은 시작해야 한다는 것이다. 지금 시작하고, 여기서 시작하고, 작게 시작해야 한다.
quotes.context: On entrepreneurship and taking action immediately without waiting for perfect conditions
quotes.contextKo: 완벽한 조건을 기다리지 않고 즉각적으로 행동하는 기업가정신에 대해

quotes.text: Everyone has an idea. But it's really about executing the idea and attracting other people to help you.
quotes.textKo: 누구나 아이디어는 있다. 하지만 진짜 중요한 것은 그 아이디어를 실행하고 당신을 도와줄 다른 사람들을 끌어들이는 것이다.
quotes.context: On the importance of execution over having ideas alone
quotes.contextKo: 단순히 아이디어를 가지는 것보다 실행의 중요성에 대해

quotes.text: Make every detail perfect and limit the number of details to perfect.
quotes.textKo: 모든 세부사항을 완벽하게 만들되, 완벽하게 만들 세부사항의 수를 제한하라.

quotes.text: My goal is to simplify complexity. I just want to build stuff that really simplifies our base human interaction.
quotes.textKo: 내 목표는 복잡성을 단순화하는 것이다. 우리의 기본적인 인간 상호작용을 정말로 단순화하는 것들을 만들고 싶다.
quotes.context: On core mission and product design philosophy
quotes.contextKo: 핵심 미션과 제품 디자인 철학에 대해

quotes.text: The strongest thing you can cultivate as an entrepreneur is to not rely on luck but cultivating an ability to recognize fortunate situations when they are occurring.
quotes.textKo: 기업가로서 배양할 수 있는 가장 강력한 것은 운에 의존하지 않는 것이지만, 운이 좋은 상황을 일어날 때 인식하는 능력을 배양하는 것이다.

recommended.title: The Art Spirit
recommended.author: Robert Henri
recommended.why: Influences Dorsey's design philosophy and minimalist approach to product creation
recommended.whyKo: 도르시의 디자인 철학과 제품 제작의 극소주의적 접근에 영향을 미침

recommended.title: The Score Takes Care of Itself
recommended.author: Bill Walsh
recommended.why: Leadership and team management insights applicable to building and running companies
recommended.whyKo: 회사 구축 및 운영에 적용할 수 있는 리더십과 팀 관리 통찰

recommended.title: Principles
recommended.author: Ray Dalio
recommended.why: First-principles thinking and systematic approach to decision-making
recommended.whyKo: 첫 원리 사고와 의사결정에 대한 체계적 접근

personalTraits.knownFor: Co-founding Twitter and revolutionizing social media and real-time communication; co-founding Square/Block and advancing mobile payments and fintech; radical minimalism and extreme wellness practices including meditation, ice baths, and fasting; unwavering commitment to Bitcoin and decentralized finance; design thinking and simplicity philosophy; long-term vision and decentralization principles
personalTraits.knownForKo: 트위터 공동 설립 및 소셜 미디어와 실시간 통신의 혁신; 스퀘어/블록 공동 설립 및 모바일 결제와 핀테크 발전; 명상, 얼음 목욕, 단식을 포함한 급진적 극소주의와 극단적 웰니스 실천; 비트코인과 분산 금융에 대한 확고한 헌신; 디자인 사고와 단순성 철학; 장기적 비전과 분산화 원칙
personalTraits.philanthropy: Committed to giving away majority of his wealth in his lifetime. Announced moving $1 billion to COVID-19 relief, girls' education, health, and universal basic income in April 2020. Donated 14 Bitcoin to support Nostr, a decentralized social networking protocol. Focus on financial inclusion and empowerment.
personalTraits.philanthropyKo: 평생 자산의 대부분을 기부할 것을 약속. 2020년 4월 COVID-19 구호, 여성 교육, 보건, 보편적 기본소득에 10억 달러 이전 발표. 분산화된 사회 네트워킹 프로토콜인 노스트르 지원을 위해 14개의 비트코인 기부. 금융 포용성과 권한 부여에 초점.
personalTraits.controversies: Criticized for divided leadership attention while running both Twitter and Square simultaneously; facing backlash for content moderation decisions including Trump account suspension; Twitter's platform challenges with misinformation, abuse, and safety issues during his tenure; allegations of absent leadership and disengagement from operations
personalTraits.controversiesKo: 트위터와 스퀘어를 동시에 운영하면서 분산된 리더십 관심으로 비판받음; 트럼프 계정 정지를 포함한 콘텐츠 검증 결정으로 반발 직면; 재임 중 트위터의 허위정보, 남용, 안전 문제에 대한 플랫폼 도전; 부재중인 리더십과 운영 이탈 혐의

pivotalMentors.name: Jim McKelvey
pivotalMentors.relationship: Boss and co-founder of Square. McKelvey was Dorsey's boss when Dorsey was a 15-year-old intern at his software company.
pivotalMentors.influence: McKelvey impressed by young Dorsey's talents, called him 'Jack the Genius.' Later co-founded Square with Dorsey after sharing story about losing a $3,000 glass faucet sale due to inability to process credit card payments.

pivotalMentors.name: Noah Glass
pivotalMentors.relationship: Co-founder of Twitter at Odeo. Glass was Odeo co-founder who gravitated toward Dorsey.
pivotalMentors.influence: Glass recognized Dorsey as 'one of the stars of the company' and came up with the name 'twttr' for Dorsey's status message idea.

pivotalMentors.name: Biz Stone
pivotalMentors.relationship: Co-founder of Twitter and early colleague at Odeo
pivotalMentors.influence: Collaborated with Dorsey to build Twitter prototype in approximately two weeks, translating the concept from inspiration to reality.

additionalInsights.dailyRoutine: Wakes early and meditates 1-2 hours daily (1 hour morning, 1 hour evening), considering it the 'biggest impact' to his mental health after 20 years of practice. Takes ice baths at 37°F (3°C) each morning for mental confidence and resilience. Eats only one meal per day on weekdays (typically dinner) and fasts from Friday evening to Sunday evening on weekends, believing this maximizes focus and improves sleep. Walks approximately 5 miles to and from work. Uses sauna in evenings. Maintains extreme discipline and minimalist lifestyle focused on clarity and presence.
keyAnecdotes: At age 14, wrote dispatch software for taxicabs and firefighting services that remained in use by companies for decades afterward.
At age 15, hacked into a large New York dispatch company's security hole to get hired—found a vulnerability on their website, emailed them how to fix it, and was offered a job within a week.
Obsessed with emergency dispatch communications as a child, inspired by listening to police scanner conversations about location and status, which became the seed for Twitter.
Posted the world's first tweet on March 21, 2006: 'just setting up my twttr,' launching social media revolution.
Built Twitter prototype with Biz Stone in just two weeks at Odeo before it became the global phenomenon.
Later acknowledged that investing too much in company tools to manage public conversation was a mistake, recognizing the company had accumulated too much power over discourse.
additionalInsights.turningPoint: The 2006 moment at Odeo when Dorsey found himself in a talented team that could help build his 2000 Twitter idea. While the initial concept came in 2000, it wasn't until 2006 at Odeo with colleagues Evan Williams, Noah Glass, and Biz Stone that the fortunate convergence of people, timing, and resources enabled him to actually build and launch Twitter, transforming how humans communicate globally.
additionalInsights.managementStyle: Minimalist, visionary, and decentralized leadership emphasizing first-principles thinking and long-term vision. Advocates for flat organizational structures where staff are empowered as creative thinkers and initiative leaders freed from bureaucratic constraints. Believes good leadership guides people to make decisions themselves rather than issuing orders from above ('If I have to make a decision, we have an organizational failure'). Emphasizes radical transparency, direct communication, and extreme discipline. Focuses on simplicity, clarity, and removing unnecessary elements—both in product design and operations. Committed to decentralization principles and protocols, particularly in advancing Bitcoin and blockchain technologies.
